question: when was the last time anyone was on the moon
answer: The last time anyone was on the moon was during the Apollo 17 mission in December 1972. The mission was crewed by astronauts Eugene Cernan, Ronald Evans, and Harrison Schmitt. They spent three days on the moon's surface, conducting scientific experiments and exploring the lunar terrain. Eugene Cernan was the last person to walk on the moon, stepping off the lunar module Challenger onto the moon's surface on December 11, 1972.
judge_reason: Provides the correct month/year (December 1972) but gives an incorrect specific date (December 11) for the last moonwalk.

question: when did the eagles win last super bowl
answer: The Philadelphia Eagles won their last Super Bowl in Super Bowl LII (52) on February 4, 2018, when they defeated the New England Patriots 41-33.
